title: Western Blot Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rtmr","text":"Western Blotting is a technique for the immunodetection of proteins using antibodies with fluorescent or chemiluminescent detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"b2s6e","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3r4sd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aov8t","text":"0.33 M sucrose","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fq7kv","text":"8 mM Hepes, pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ed5h","text":"Laemli SDS boiling buffer (Sigma) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c089s","text":"9-12% SDS-polyacrilamide gel ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1540","text":"polyvinylidene difluoride membrane (Amersham Biosciences, Piscataway, NJ) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ffm4l","text":"5% non-fat dry milk ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6mj3o","text":"TBST","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7upal","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare protein extracts as previously described.

Homogenize tissues in lysis buffer ( 0.33 Molarity (M) sucrose/ 8 millimolar (mM) Hepes, pH 7.4 and protease inhibitors) and quantify them using the BCA protein determination method (Bio-Rad, Hercules, CA).
Dilute protein samples to equivalent volumes containing 20 µg of protein and boil in an equal volume of Laemli SDS boiling buffer (Sigma) for 00:10:00 .
Load samples into a 9-12% SDS-polyacrilamide gel and separate it by electrophoresis for 03:00:00 at 100 V.
Transfer the proteins to polyvinylidene difluoride membrane (Amersham Biosciences, Piscataway, NJ) for 01:30:00 at 300 mA.
After blocking of nonspecific binding with 5% non-fat dry milk in TBST, probe the membranes with primary antibodies and process.
Perform densitometric analysis using ImageQuantity One.

Normalize data to β-actin, normalize values of phosphorylated GSK-3β (pTyr 216 GSK-3β); phosphorylated α-syn (pSer 129 α-syn) and phosphorylated tau (pSer 396 tau) to total GSK-3β, α-syn, and tau, respectively, before statistical analysis of variance and express values as percent changes (%) of WT controls.

Dashed lines (in white) indicate discontinuous bands (nonsequential lanes) taken from the same blot, at the same molecular weight (mass – kDa) in order to better represent the mean signal from all values (5-6 individual blots/genotype/treatment) for that particular group. Corresponding control bands (loading controls) match experimental bands.
creator: carmela giachino
